title: SkinApp Training Final Assessment
text:
Final Assessment

You will now complete a comprehensive assessment covering all modules:

  • Module 1: Project Overview
  • Module 2: Clinical Photography
  • Module 3: Informed Consent
  • Module 4: Privacy & Anonymization
  • Module 5: Safeguarding

You need to achieve 80% (16/20) to pass. You can retake this assessment if needed.

params.question: What is the primary target number for expert-verified leprosy images in this phase?
If the AI gives a 95% confidence score, what is its primary role?
Why is it a project priority to recruit high-quality clinical images from diverse regions like Africa and Asia?

answers.text: Because leprosy only exists in these regions
To ensure the AI algorithm is trained on a globally representative dataset, preventing diagnostic bias
Because smartphone cameras work better in these climates

params.question: Why does the SkinApp AI need images of ALL clinical forms of leprosy (e.g., Indeterminate, Tuberculoid, Lepromatous)?

answers.text: To ensure the AI can detect the full clinical spectrum, including early and subtle forms
Because research grants require a certain number of files for each category
To make the app more colorful for the end-user

params.question: Which optical technique ensures correct texture capture for AI?

answers.text: Tapping the lesion to focus and moving 10-15cm back
Using the camera flash for extra detail

params.question: Why should medical rulers be EXCLUDED from AI training photos?

answers.text: They take up too much file space
The AI may learn the ruler's presence instead of the lesion

params.question: At what angle should clinical images be taken relative to the skin surface?

answers.text: Perpendicular (90 degrees) to prevent perspective distortion
Dynamic angles to show the shape

params.question: What device feature MUST be disabled before photographing?
Who is the primary contact for patient privacy queries and complaints?
A patient can withdraw consent at any time, even after the photo is uploaded.
params.tip: Correct! Consent is an ongoing process.

params.question: What is the best way to anonymize a lesion on a patient's mouth?

answers.text: Crop tightly or use clinical masking to cover the eyes and identifying profile
Anonymization isn't needed for medical skin

params.question: What must be physically removed from the framing before the shutter is pressed?

answers.text: Nothing, software will fix it later

params.question: Is 'informed consent' just a signed piece of paper?
params.tip: No, it is an ongoing clinical dialogue.

params.question: What is the protocol for photographing suspected lesions on genitalia?

answers.text: Include them if consent is signed
Photographs of genitalia are strictly not accepted for this project
Must use a 4K camera for these

params.question: For lesions on the buttocks or breasts, what is required?

answers.text: Strategic medical draping to expose ONLY the lesion

params.question: What does the ethical pillar of 'Beneficence' mean in this context?

answers.text: Acting in the best interest of the patient/society

params.question: For a 15-year-old, besides parental consent, what additional layer is needed?
Why do we record Age and Gender in the AI training metadata?

answers.text: Because demographics influence skin appearance/pattern recognition

params.question: Why is 'dappled light' (patchy shadows) rejected?

answers.text: The AI can confuse shadows with disease pigmentation
It increases the image file size

params.question: What is the most effective way to prevent 'motion blur' in clinical images?

answers.text: Brace your arms against your body or a hard surface
Take the photo while the patient is moving

overallFeedback.feedback: You did not pass. You need 80% to complete this training. Please review the modules and try again.
Congratulations! You have successfully completed the SkinApp Training Course. You are now prepared to contribute to global leprosy care technology.
